reviews.review_msg: Easily the best prof. I've had at sfu. Super articulate and progressed my learning journey more than any other phil. prof. or program

Dr Lauro makes his lectures engaging! Some of the examples he uses caught me off guard and made me pay attention when I drifted off. My TA didn't give me specific feedback on my essay draft - Dr Lauro was easily approachable and made sure my TA gave better feedback on the next assignment.

Professor Lauro explained things clearly and well, he also had a number of funny moments through his lectures. Essays were clear about what they would be graded on. My only complaint is that he talked rather fast, which I didn't mind too much, he's got a lot of slides to cover after all, and he didn't post the slides until after lecture.

Greg Lauro is an amazing teacher who answered every question I asked him was very approachable made the lectures entertaining to go to and is just a good guy. That being said he is not the one who marks your paper. I recommend going to tutorials as even though they don't give any credits your TA can suck and be very picky about writing. take again

Definitely one of the best profs I've had at SFU. His lectures are filled with sufficient examples and clear definitions of all the concepts and terms such that you don't need to take notes, just simply watch and learn. Aside from that, he's a patient prof and very calm. The essays are simple which makes it very easy to actually show what you know.

Dr. Lauro tries his best to make the 3 hours lecture as tolerable as he can by making the lectures more engaging between peers. The textbook used is quite a hard read and the content is all over the place, but Lauro clarifies it a lot better in his slides.

Ik it's only week 3 as of writing this, but his 3-hour lectures are very engaging, he explains abstract concepts in a very clear way that makes me understand everything. I'm not even a philosophy major but this might be literally my favorite class right now

Greg makes complicated concepts easy to understand and is very accessible. Class was graded on 6 problem sets (can work in groups) and a final worth 20%. I found him very clear and easy to understand. Though, he did lecture for 2 hours straight, which after a long day makes it hard to pay attention all the way through.
Gregory was a great prof with a good sense of humour. He had a passion for epistemology and he explained concepts clearly when the textbook couldn't.

He was the author of the required book for the course which he offered for free and was pretty much enough to get through the course without really needing to attending lectures. You have to attend a min number of tutorials but recommend attending all of them. The last few homeworks and quizzes were kind of tough but overall a manageable course.
